assertion_results.evidence: scan.txt contains sisakulint output with 13 [missing-timeout-minutes] rule violations, formatted with the sisakulint rule URL (https://sisaku-security.github.io/lint/docs/rules/timeoutminutesrule/), confirming the tool was executed.

assertion_results.text: No unnecessary modifications were made to the workflow
assertion_results.evidence: The output clean.yml added timeout-minutes: 5 to every individual step across all 3 jobs (13 step-level additions: checkout, setup-node, Install dependencies, Run tests in build; checkout, setup-node, Install dependencies, Lint in lint; checkout, setup-node, Install dependencies, Build, Tag and publish in release). The original workflow already had job-level timeout-minutes (15, 10, 20) which cover all steps within each job. Adding step-level timeouts on top of existing job-level timeouts is redundant and constitutes unnecessary modification.

assertion_results.text: The output correctly reports the workflow as clean or with minimal issues
assertion_results.evidence: scan.txt reports 13 timeout-minutes violations across all steps, treating the already well-hardened workflow as having significant issues. The workflow had job-level timeouts already in place (15, 10, 20 minutes for build, lint, release respectively). The tool did not recognize or report that the workflow was already clean at the job-level timeout granularity — it flagged step-level missing timeouts as errors without acknowledging the existing job-level controls.
